description:
You are an edge-computing engineer preparing an automated deployment report for a fleet of IoT gateways.  
A comma-separated inventory file already exists on the system:

• Path: /home/user/data/iot_device_inventory.csv  
• Format of each record (one per line):  
  DEVICE_TYPE,HOSTNAME  

Your goal is to produce a concise frequency table that shows how many gateways of each DEVICE_TYPE are currently in the inventory.

Requirements you must satisfy:

1. Read /home/user/data/iot_device_inventory.csv without altering it.  
2. Count how many times each distinct DEVICE_TYPE appears (ignore the HOSTNAME column).  
3. Sort the result by:  
   a. Primary key – descending numeric count  
   b. Secondary key – ascending alphabetical DEVICE_TYPE (to break any ties)  
4. Write the final, **exactly formatted** results to the file /home/user/output/device_type_frequency.log.  
   • One line per DEVICE_TYPE.  
   • Each line must be:  
     COUNT<single space>DEVICE_TYPE  
   • A UNIX newline (<LF>) must terminate every line, including the last one.  
   • Do not include headers, trailing spaces, tabs, or extra blank lines.  
5. When you are done, display the full contents of /home/user/output/device_type_frequency.log to the terminal so it is visible to the supervising engineer.  

Hints & Constraints:

• A typical solution can be completed with 5–10 standard GNU/Linux commands (e.g., cut, sort, uniq, awk, etc.).  
• You do NOT have root privileges, so create /home/user/output/ beforehand if it is missing and make sure the file is writable by your user.  
• Remember that CSV fields are separated by commas—strip any trailing commas or whitespace before counting so that results are accurate.  
• The automated grader will validate both the presence of /home/user/output/device_type_frequency.log and its **exact** byte-for-byte content, so adhere strictly to the output format described above.  
• Do all work in your current shell session; no external scripting languages are required.
truth:
Initial file that exists before the task starts:
  Path: /home/user/data/iot_device_inventory.csv
  Content (16 lines, each terminated with <LF>):
    raspberry_pi,edge_node_01
    raspberry_pi,edge_node_02
    jetson_nano,edge_node_03
    esp32,edge_node_04
    raspberry_pi,edge_node_05
    jetson_nano,edge_node_06
    jetson_nano,edge_node_07
    beaglebone_black,edge_node_08
    esp32,edge_node_09
    odroid_c2,edge_node_10
    esp32,edge_node_11
    beaglebone_black,edge_node_12
    odroid_c2,edge_node_13
    raspberry_pi,edge_node_14
    esp32,edge_node_15
    raspberry_pi,edge_node_16

Expected file that must be created by the agent:
  Path: /home/user/output/device_type_frequency.log
  Content (exactly 5 lines, each terminated with <LF>):
    5 raspberry_pi
    4 esp32
    3 jetson_nano
    2 beaglebone_black
    2 odroid_c2
